title: Srila Prabhupada Stories 4: Swami drops a bomb!
speaker: HG Chanchalapathi Dasa

segments.text: Hare Krishna
Namaste Saraswate Deve Gauravani Pracharine
Nirvishesha Shunyavadi Paschatya Deshatarine
Jaya Shri Krishna Chetanya Prabhu Nityananda
Shri Advaita Gadadhara Shri Vasadi Gaurabhatta Vrindha
Namaste Saraswate Deve Gauravani Pracharine
Hare Krishna Hare Krishna Krishna Krishna Hare Hare
Hare Rama Hare Rama Rama Rama Hare Hare
Very happy to be with all of you
to share more stories about Srila Prabhupada's wonderful life.
Some years ago I visited one Madhava Sanskrit scholar in Bangalore.
And I wanted to talk to him about the issue in ISKCON and major issue in ISKCON
about initiation.
So.
So.
I went to him and asked him, what is Diksha or initiation according to Madhva tradition?
So he said, he thought for a moment and he said, Diksha, which Diksha are you talking
about?
So then I asked him, oh you mean to say which Diksha, there are more than one Diksha?
Then he said, yes, we have a Matha Diksha, where you get initiated into a certain Matha,
you are affiliated to a certain Matha.
So we have something like that.
We have Ashram Diksha, when somebody enters Sanya's Ashram, he takes Diksha and so that's
called, there is a Diksha around that.
Then he said, there is something called Vratha Diksha, when you take a Vratha, when you perform
a Vratha, a vow, that for the next 21 days I will do this or something like that.
There are, there is a Diksha where you get initiated into that Vratha.
And like this, there are many kinds of Diksha, which Diksha are you referring to?
Because Diksha,
varies from tradition to tradition and a particular situation or a certain purpose.
So then I started thinking, yeah, that's true.
Diksha is an ancient Sanskrit Vedic cultural concept, Vedic culture, concept in Vedic culture.
But what are we referring to when we talk in ISKCON about Diksha, Diksha Guru, who is
the Diksha Guru and all of these kinds of things?
Then I started reflecting, we know how Srila Prabhupada representing a very ancient traditions
beginning from Krishna to Narada Krishna to Brahma to Narada to Vyasa and then the Acharyas
and then Chaitanya Mahaprabhu.
And he was representing that very, very ancient tradition.
So I started reflecting.
Then I started thinking.
On a very important and spiritual tradition.
And now he was going to America.
And he was founding a worldwide organization.
And in the context of this worldwide organization, Srila Prabhupada was introducing all of these
important spiritual concepts.
And we know Srila Prabhupada, an empowered Acharyas.
Pure devotees.
Absolutely.
Krishna doing all of these things. So when Srila Prabhupada introduced Diksha
in ISKCON, what did he have in mind? What was the meaning? What was the purport?
What was the intent? And what was the practice that Srila Prabhupada was
introducing? Because all that is relevant. We are talking about problem, about Diksha,
Diksha Guru in ISKCON. Not in isolation of ISKCON. It is in our institutional life,
it is in our spiritual life, in the institution that Srila Prabhupada founded. So we can't take
some esoteric, some Sanskrit shloka or some other tradition and try to understand
what was, what is Diksha in ISKCON. And when we come back to this,
I will start this. So when we come back, I will start this. So when we come back,
I will start this. So when we come back, I will start this. So when we come back,
in our context, in ISKCON's context. So the best way is to understand what Srila Prabhupada had
in mind when he talked about Diksha or even more how Srila Prabhupada articulated
the meaning and the understanding of Diksha and how he implemented the practice of Diksha
in ISKCON. So what is significant is then I began to think that
let's see how Srila Prabhupada introduced Diksha the first time he did and maybe several times
after that when Srila Prabhupada presided over the Diksha ceremony, how would he describe that
or how would Srila Prabhupada talk about that during lectures or conversations
and such?
So it's interesting how Srila Prabhupada introduced the first Diksha ceremony,
initiation ceremony. It happened in September of 1966.
Let's give a little background to it. Srila Prabhupada had established the first ISKCON temple
and
he had incorporated the ISKCON Society in July of 1966.
That's when Srila Prabhutada had a fixed place to stay. Earlier He was moving from one place to
another, sometimes sharing a room with another, sharing a loft with someone else and sharing
the premises with another Yoga teacher and all of those activities and other things.
kind of things. Now he had a place for himself and that was 26 2nd Avenue which
became the famous first ISKCON temple in ISKCON. So Srila Prabhupada had this
storefront. As an Indian when I used to read Srila Prabhupada's biography that
Prabhupada had a storefront, had rented a storefront and converted that into a
temple I used to wonder what is a storefront you know we for Indians in
Indian English language and our urban understanding we don't know what is a
storefront and this was in the 1980s you know at least maybe now modern day young
Americans, young Indians educated Indians may have an understanding but I
definitely didn't have. I used to wonder what's a storefront but then when you
read something you may not understand but you keep going on so that was what
used to happen to me till at some point I visited the storefront on 26 second
Avenue so it's a it's a store it's a shop there are many shops all around and
this is also one shop if you use the Indian English language so it was a big
hall it could be like a you know some kind of any kind of a store that is
possible there and it was narrow and longish and then at the back there was
one washroom and then there was another door there at the back the front is a
big door as wide as the width of the door of the store and for some reason I
also not figured out Americans use the word storefront
for that and so Srila Prabhupada had rented that place and he would conduct
programs there where these young Americans who are looking for new life
new meaning of life trying to understand Eastern philosophy Indian philosophy
Buddhism and who are also experimenting on drugs for some higher consciousness
experience all of these kind of young people would come there
and Srila Prabhupada would conduct bhajans kirtans and he would give a
class and Prabhupad was living in an apartment on the first floor just behind
So Prabhupada would go through that rear door and then it would go to a courtyard
and there is a flight of steps which would go to one flat in that apartment
one and that place was so large so the room would be come up you know to their
back one and that place would be before them and the rest was just in aultimate size
that's where Prabhupada used to live. So Prabhupada used to live there, cook there,
he would bathe there, there was a washroom and all of that and he would come here and conduct
classes, kirtans and all of that and would go back. This was the kind of a situation that
existed at that time. So this happened in July, July 13th, 14th when the incorporation of ISKCON
happened. Now we are talking about July, August, September, not very long, just about two and a
half, three months, two months, little over two months. So Srila Prabhupada would conduct classes
and then in one of the classes and there were a few people, young American boys and girls who
were coming and they were trying to understand what Swamiji, they used to call Srila Prabhupada
Swamiji at that time, what Swamiji?
is going to tell. And they would go meet Swamiji in the apartment also, where there would be a
little more intimate conversations. They would ask questions and ask Swamiji about his life,
about India and all their questions about philosophy and all of those kind of intimate
discussions would happen up there in the apartment. And this was more formal and many
newcomers would come. And Srila Prabhupada, we talked,
about how he would go from here to the Tompkins Square Park and have kirtans on Sundays and all of
those kind of things. So one day, actually he had spoken informally in the apartment, it looks like,
and about initiation and his plan to conduct initiation. So one day devotees asked Srila
Prabhupada, Swamiji, what is initiation?
Important question for me to answer the Madhva scholar. And Srila Prabhupada, a brilliant
communicator of Krishna conscious philosophy, his answer was, I will tell you later. So
it was brilliant answer because Prabhupada did answer but increased the level of curiosity
for all the young people.
So it was brilliant answer because Prabhupada did answer but increased the level of curiosity for all the young people.
who are coming there.
So they all were waiting to know what is Swamiji going to tell about initiation, but
he said, I will tell you at the right time.
I will tell you later.
So they were even more curious and then Prabhupada had to know, had pointed out September 8th,
1966, is Krishna Janmashtami, the day Krishna appeared on earth 5000 years ago.
years ago. So very important. Srila Prabhupada is telling about Krishna and we are supposed to
become devotees of Krishna, bhakti to Krishna and the movement is called, the society is called
International Society for Krishna Consciousness. So Krishna is so important and Krishna appearance
day. So Prabhupada said, on that day we should chant more, we should hear about Krishna
and we should fast till midnight. At midnight Krishna appeared and we will have a feast then.
And so you can imagine for these young Americans, boys and girls who are coming there,
just given off drug, some of them, some of them still taking drugs maybe,
some of them had become vegetarians, some of them had not yet become vegetarians probably,
all kinds of young American boys and girls and they are hearing from Swamiji about all these
things and then they are told that they have to fast whole day. And then Prabhupada was very
considerate. He said, if you find fasting becoming too difficult, you can go up to the apartment,
there I'll have some fruits.
You can have a fruit there. So this was the concession that Srila Prabhupada had given.
So on the day of Krishna Janmashtami, Srila Prabhupada came down and he conducted the class.
Of course there was another interesting thing that happened on that particular day.
Usually Srila Prabhupada would speak on Bhagavad Gita and at that time Srila Prabhupada's own Bhagavad
Gita was available. There was Bhagavatam available, but he would be speaking on Bhagavad Gita verses
and he would use Dr. Radhakrishnan's Bhagavad Gita, Oxford University Press publication.
So Prabhupada said Dr. Radhakrishnan's Bhagavad Gita was 90% accurate, but Prabhupada had warned
that somehow in Dr. Radhakrishnan's explanations, he would go into
personal understanding of the absolute truth. So I won't go into that analysis,
I will do that another occasion and how Srila Prabhupada explained that.
There are very very interesting stories around that, we'll keep it for another occasion.
So Prabhupada would read from the Bhagavad Gita of Dr. Radhakrishnan and then he would explain.
But then on that particular day, Prabhupada was actually working on his own Bhagavad Gita.
He was typing the manuscripts, all that was also going on. So one of the young Americans
students who was, we don't, we may not call him disciple at this point of time,
they were not formal disciples, they were just eager to know and associate with Swamiji,
they were coming, they were inquisitive, they were curious, they were very sincere,
they really wanted to know what's all this about and they were coming to understand more. So they
asked Swamiji,
can you read from your manuscript? We have heard that you are preparing the Bhagavad Gita.
So Swami Prabhupada was a little amused and very happy to hear that.
And so he had one of the boys go up to the apartment, bring the bundle and they brought
the bundle and then Prabhupada read for the next one hour translations and the purports
that he had compiled for a long, for a certain number of, about an hour or so.
And then at the end of the talk, Prabhupada said, and Prabhupada had announced already,
the next day after Janmashtami, September 8th, 1966 was Janmashtami, September 9th
was going to be the initiation day. Srila Prabhupada didn't tell what is significant about
the next day of Janmashtami. We all know now today,
that was the day Srila Prabhupada appeared. He didn't talk about that one, at least it looks
like from the biographical information that I have read about. And it was supposed to be the
next day of Krishna Janmashtami. So Srila, so everybody knew that next day was going to be the
initiation, the beads were bought. And Srila Prabhupada said you should get beads from a store
and told,
some of the women who were coming, one of them was Jan, Mike's girlfriend, and also supposed to
be, who have given their name for the first initiation. So Prabhupada said, or rather we will
say, Swamiji said, women are patient, they have patience, and they should make the beads. And so
Jan had taken it upon herself, maybe someone else also helped her, and she was going to make the
beads for her.
And the beads would have 108, the mala would have the 108 beads, beads bought from a store
in America, some American store. And so it was not tulasi beads and all of those kind, you know where
you don't have those kind of things at that time.
So at the end of the class on Janmashtami, Krishna Janmashtami day, Srila Prabhupada says, now I will explain what is
initiation. All these young boys and girls, they leaned forward, they were alert, they wanted to
hear, because the next day some of them had given their names and they're going to go through that.
What is initiation? There was speculation, discussion among them. It looks like some kind
of a passage of rites. Oh what is that? So let's wait for Swamiji to explain. And now Srila Prabhupada is going to explain.
One more thing before I give what Prabhupada said. There was another thing that was happening
during those days. Those days when Prabhupada was, Swamiji was giving classes and he would have
discussions, questions and answers, invariably some of them had read about some Indian philosophy,
Buddhism philosophy, some Indian Hinduism, Hindu guru, yoga teacher and all of those
kind of things. And they would have heard those things and they would come and ask Swamiji questions,
because they knew Swamiji was very scholarly, Swamiji was very authentic, he always referred to the
Shastra, scriptures, the Vedic literatures, the previous Acharyas. And Swamiji looked so effulgent
and so confident and there is a certain kind of a, you know, you
can trust Swamiji. So there was a kind of a confidence in Swamiji and so they
would ask him those kind of questions that they would come across. So very
often the theme would be, the questions would be, I have heard that we should
meditate on God and by doing meditation on God gradually we will realize God and
we will become God and that way there is oneness between us and God and so we
become God. So this was the common very often popular theme and question among
and should be asked.
Srila Prabhupada would strongly speak about this.
This is a philosophy that's not explained in the Gita and not explained in the Vaishnava
tradition.
According to the Vaishnava tradition, according to the Bhagavad Gita, we are all part and
parcel of Krishna.
We are servant of Krishna.
Krishna is the Supreme Master.
Prabhupada often quoted verses from the Upanishads, Nithyo Nithyanam, Chetanas Chetananam, Eko
Bahunam, Yo Vidha Dati Kamar, Katha Upanishad, where it says that the living entities are
many and there is the Lord who is one, both of them are eternal and they are eternally
the same.
The Lord will eternally be the Lord and the living entity part and parcel will eternally
be the part and parcel.
The Lord is conscious, the living entity is conscious, Chetanas Chetananam, Nithyo Nithyanam,
Chetanas Chetananam, eternal and eternal, Eko Bahunam, Yo Vidha Dati Kamar and that
one is the provider of everything that the many conscious eternal entities want or desire.
So one is the supplier, one is the receiver.
The supplier, the receiver cannot become the supplier, he is always eternally the receiver.
So these kind of ideas Srila Prabhupada had often talked about and then Prabhupada would
often say.
If somebody says, I am God, you know, it's an absurd statement.
I realize God, I meditate on God and I become God.
You know what God is? God controls the planetary movements.
And I start controlling the planetary movements.
What kind of an absurdity that is?
Is the sun going to listen to me?
Unless I am some insane guy thinking that I have become God,
now I have God realized and I'm controlling all the stars and the planets and the moon
and all of those kind of things.
How absurd it is.
So Prabhupada would strongly speak against this kind of an understanding and say,
no, from the Bhagavad Gita, from the teachings of the Vedic literatures,
we understand we are not God.
We are servant of God.
We are of the quality of God, but not God.
God is God.
We are always his part, amsa, mamai vamsho jiva loke, jivabhuta sanatana.
This is Krishna's instruction in the Gita.
Sanatana we are amsa, eternally we are amsa.
And his amsa, he is the purna, he is the full.
So this is the philosophy Prabhupada often would talk.
And then sometimes, very often these questions would come about.
Someone would come and declare that I have been practicing yoga for the last one year.
And I have become God.
You know, all those kind of things that these kind of people would come and declare.
So Prabhupada would strongly say,
if someone says he is God, he is not God.
He is the opposite.
Not G-O-D.
He is D-O-G.
You know, these kind of very, very strong themes that Prabhupada would talk about.
In all of his classes.
And so these young Americans, they would hear about all these things and then oh, Swamiji says that we are not God.
We are actually servant of God and all of those kind of things.
So it was one of the popular themes that Srila Prabhupada had repeatedly
stressed in his classes, in his discussions and question answers and all of that.
And these young Americans who are at that time coming, they were familiar with that.
Now coming back, Janma Ashtami day, morning class is over, next day is going to be the initiation and Srila Prabhupada had said he's now going to tell what is initiation.
Everyone is curious, waiting to hear what Swamiji will say is initiation.
Then Prabhupada pauses for a moment.
Clears his throat.
And then he says, initiation means the spiritual master accepts the student as his disciple.
And agrees to deliver him.
And the student accepts the spiritual master.
And agrees to worship him.
And agrees to worship him.
him as God. There was silence. Everyone was here. In the Srimad Bhagavatam, it is
described. This is Krishna speaking. Krishna is
telling how an Acharya, a spiritual Master, must be regarded. Then Swamiji
asked, any questions? It was just still just descending. They were just just
absorbing what Swamiji had just defined as initiation. There was silence.
Swamiji rose from his seat, turned around and walked to the door, opened the door
door, walked out, shut the door behind him and he went away. And these young Americans,
they were looking at each other. Did you hear that? And they said, yes. Another one said,
one said, Swamiji just dropped a bomb. Another said, yes, it was mind blowing. And then they
started discussing. So does that mean Swamiji is God? We have to worship him as God? What does
this mean? Maybe he is God. We didn't understand. You know, maybe he is God who has come from India.
And someone said, no, it can't be. Always Swamiji has been telling, if somebody says I am God,
he is not God. He is not G-O-D, he is D-O-G. So this was a popular theme. They had heard
about it. They had heard about it. They had heard about it. They had heard about it. They had heard
about it. Now what is Swamiji saying? So they started again discussing, not much time. Then
someone said, maybe then I am not ready for the initiation tomorrow. I should drop out from that
list. So all these kind of discussions were happening. So then they had among them, there was
one of them who was considered to be very intelligent, very sharp and little older also. And
that was Keith.
Keith was
for some reason in a hospital.
So they all went to the hospital and they asked,
they discussed, Hover, Vaali and a few others.
They went to the hospital and they discussed. You know what Swamiji said? Swamiji said initiation
means that spiritual master accepts this disciple, the student and agrees to deliver and the student
should accept him.
Guinquис
as the spiritual master and agree to worship him as God, to be considered, to be understood,
to be regarded like me. So this is Krishna's instruction in the Srimad Bhagavatam,
how the spiritual master has to be regarded.
And the spiritual master should not be regarded as a
means an ordinary human being who appears in this world, lives for some time and dies. He is known
as a , one who dies like any other conditioned souls. means conception,
understanding, go and ask and ask for a clarification. And then they all discussed
among them, they said yes we should go and ask for a clarification. So they came back, they went to
Swamiji's room in the apartment up there on the first floor and then they asked Swamiji,
Swamiji you just said that we must decide. In the Srimad Bhagavatam it is described,
Aacharya maam vijaniyan naavamanyetakarhi chit. This is Krishna speaking. Krishna is telling
how an Aacharya, a spiritual master must be regarded. Krishna is saying Aacharya maam
vijaniyan, maam vijaniyan to be considered, to be understood, to be regarded like me.
Aacharya maam vijaniyan naavamanyetakarhi chit. He should never be disrespected.
So this is Krishna's instruction in the Srimad Bhagavatam, how the spiritual master has to be
regarded. And the spiritual master should not be regarded as a
celibate . So this marriage...
Tenным and iya krishan ira sarkum,という vereen padmanin am pitvah reethve empyarak

ailsa dhyana, r knot, a savage planet, and a fearless erlebusa as a vedasahda.

take birth here, we live for some time and we die. Anacharya is not to be regarded like
that. Never to be disrespected. Should not be considered as an ordinary
mortal. And never to be envied because such a spiritual master. The spiritual master is a
representative of all the devatas. The spiritual master, Prabhupada translated this as, now they
came. Actually what Srila Prabhupada was doing, this is wonderful thing that Prabhupada was doing.
Okay.
Okay.
Okay.
Okay.
When he in this wonderful, short, crisp articulation of what is initiation. In those
few English sentences, Prabhupada had packed in volumes of meaning explained in the Vedic
literature. Acharya Vishwanath Chakravarti Thakura had also described,
Sakshad Haritvena Samasta Shastra hi, Updastata Bhavyata eva Satbhi, and so on. So, it's one of the
important foundational principles. I must go forward quickly. So, Srila Prabhupada had described
this as the initiation. The spiritual master accepts the disciple and agrees to deliver.
Okay.
What is the meaning of Deliver? That is also very nicely explained in the
Acharyas which we sing every day in the morning in our temples.
Yahara Prasade Bhai Yeh Bhava Toriyai Krishna Prapti Ho Yahahoy Te
It is by the mercy of the spiritual master one can cross over the material
world. Yahara Prasade Bhai Yeh Bhava Toriyai. Bhava means material world, the
world of birth and death. Yeh Bhava Toriyai Krishna Prapti Ho Yahahoy Te. One can
cross over the material world and he can deliver Krishna to the disciple. This is
the meaning of Deliverance and it happens by the mercy of the spiritual
master.
This is what the spiritual master is agreeing. I will do that to you. I will
deliver you from this material world and I will deliver Krishna to you. Provided
what is that the disciple has to do? The disciple accepts the spiritual
master and agrees to worship him as God or as good as God,
as a representative of God with the same respect due to God.
Because he is a confidential servant of God. So this was the wonderful
explanation Srila Prabhupada gave on that day and then the next morning and all
these there were ten men and one woman. They all joined. It didn't happen in the
storefront. It happened in the first floor of that house, the room, on the
living room where Prabhupada was sitting was living there and there Prabhupada had created a mound of
earth and then someone had brought orange crate you know wooden crates are
there they had brought that one broken that up and that was supposed to be the
wood you know in India when we do yagna we get special Samit wood there what
can Swamiji get he could the best he could get was orange crate broken that
up and then Swamiji had arranged and and there were some bananas and then some
flowers and all the decoration and then Swamiji told all those everybody was
sitting around each other and none of them were shaved up they were not even
wearing dhoti kurta they were in their American attire and they sat there and
then Swamiji said we will all chant and then they all chanted one round Hare
Krishna mantra and then Swamiji said started chanting oh all of you repeat
after me and they were all you know this young they were all there
figuring it out.
figuring out what's going on here. Then Swamiji said,
Om, chant after me and everyone said Om.
Apavitraha, everyone for
first time hearing all these complex Sanskrit words.
Om apavitraha pavitrova sarvavastho gatopiva
yasmaret pundari kaksham bahyantara
abhyantara sucihi sri vishnu.
Made them chant all those kind of things. They were trying to figure out and
then Swamiji said, now one by one can come
and then he would give hand over the mala
and then he would give them a name. This was
another thing. And then Swamiji said, you will get a new name.
And the name will end, it will be a name of Krishna
and the name will end with Dasa or
Dasi for men or women, which means you are a servant of Krishna.
This was all new for these young Americans. It must
have been a wonderful experience. And then after that Prabhupada
Swamiji gave names. Hovar became Hayagriva Dasa.
Vali became
Umapati Dasa. Mike
Mike became Mukunda Dasa. Jan
One, the lone American woman who was there,
she became Janaki Devi Dasi. So like this
all of them got the name and then still Srila Prabhupada, Swamiji lit them,
lit the fire, sacrificial fire and then
Swamiji would chant some mantras and then Swaha, then they would
throw all those different grains into the fire
and the whole ceremony and then everybody was wondering with
billowing smoke. Oh my God, will the fire department
show up now? Will the firemen, if they come and see, what are we going to tell them?
And everybody was looking at each other and were worried. Swamiji was not
concerned. He went on with his Swaha and the chanting and all of that
and then it all ended. Everyone
was, you know, it was like a new experience and they would call each
other very self-consciously with their new names. They were trying to figure
out and then all of them at the end of the ceremony then
there it was and Prabhupada gave a small talk, Swamiji gave a talk and again
he explained what it means to worship him, regard him as God and
as good as God, respect
due to God and all of those kind of things and not God,
Guru is not God, the spiritual master is a servant of God, all those
clarifications and then everybody left.
Swamiji was alone to clean up the whole place.
Just see the thing that Srila Prabhupada was
doing and
ten, eleven, few of them and then those devotees
took initiation. There were many more who were just sitting and watching and
all of that and Swamiji had to clean up
everything. So
this was the first initiation story.
Then
we all know now there is first initiation
and Srila Prabhupada called this the Harinam Diksha,
Harinam initiation and after a few
years, it was 1968,
so this was the first, first initiation that happened.
Now we will talk about the short story about the first
second initiation. So once
again it was 1968, now
66 to 68, two years have passed.
Prabhupada, Swamiji has conducted many first initiations by now
and there have been many, few temples. There is temple in New York,
there is temple in Boston, there is temple in San Francisco, there is one
temple in Montreal, Canada and
I am not too sure, maybe Hamburg, Germany. So already
Swamiji and his disciples have gone and started
few temples. Now in 1968,
Swamiji is in Boston and now
he says he is going to introduce the second
initiation and those who have taken first initiation
they are going to take second initiation. Once again everyone is curious
what is the second initiation and again Swamiji says I will tell you at the right time
and they are all curious and then on a certain date
and then Swamiji says the second initiation is
only for men. So that was
a very disturbing thing for the women disciples
who have taken and they were thinking what is this, why is Swamiji
doing this, this is a kind of a discrimination and they were
really upset about it. They felt slighted, they felt
discriminated and they were very upset that why
is Swamiji doing this and there were actually only two women at that time
in Boston. One was Govinda Devi Dasi
another was Jadurani. Both of them very
very serious, very committed, very devoted
to Swamiji, ready to do anything for Swamiji and
but Swamiji said it is only for men
because that was the policy of the Gaviyamat and that was
generally that is a tradition in India. The second initiation was
called the Brahmana initiation and that is the initiation where
you will get the Gayatri Mantra and it is very well established in
India. Gayatri Mantra only men chant. What is this women
chanting Gayatri? That is unheard of and that was the policy of
Gaviyamat as far as I have understood. That is what I have understood
and that is what Srila Prabhupada announced too.
So on the day of second initiation it
was happening in Boston. There was one
student from Boston University, divinity
school. He was studying religion and he was studying Indian religion.
A young student, a young fellow, maybe he was doing a scholar,
maybe he was doing his PhD or something like that. And then
so Swamiji said he can be invited as the chief guest
during the second. You know, Prabhupada was doing all kinds of things
just to get people to come and hear and they should hear about
Krishna. Maybe their hearts will change. Prabhupada was
you know, he found different ways to get. So
he called him Professor Matthews. He was not actually a professor. Prabhupada would call him Professor Matthews.
And then he would say no, no, no Swamiji I am not yet a professor.
And so Matthews was
maybe he was a doctoral student or something. He was called as the
special guest during the ceremony. And then once again
a sacrificial fire and all that was conducted. And then
Swamiji spoke about second initiation. And he said it was actually
my spiritual master who introduced this. And
my spiritual master would give the Brahmana
diksha to even people who are not born in Brahminical
families. Because this is actually the true Vedic
tradition. And so anybody who has
acquired some right guna and karma has
undergone certain purification rites. And if he is serious
in spiritual life can take second initiation and which is the Brahmana
initiation will be given Gayathri mantra.
So then Matthews asked Swamiji
does that mean they have to take some special vows? Are there any special
vows that these candidates who are taking the second initiation?
Swamiji said no, there is no special vow. They have to chant the Gayathri
mantra that will be given to them. And then do they have to fast
for something? No, Swamiji said no, there is no special fasting or anything.
They just have to chant the special mantras. And so
Swamiji conducted the fire sacrifice once again.
And again there was smoke and all this chanting going on.
You know generally the neighbours of Iskand,
we become very unpopular among the neighbours of our temples.
And here too, the neighbour was a landlord and the landlord's
wife
and she was an alcoholic woman
and then she would get very upset with the devotees
and what they do. And now she is hearing, she is seeing some smoke coming
and then hearing all these chants. And while this chanting is going on
she comes rushing into the house, into the temple
where the devotees are conducting this ceremony.
She opens the door forcibly, walks in and then she looks
her hair is all dishevelled and her garment is
also dishevelled and then she is looking at them.
And then she is very upset and she is drunk obviously.
And she is very upset and she looks at everybody there and then she is just
God damn this house! And then she turns around and
goes back, slams the door and walks away.
Swamiji is very peaceful. He looks at her, he hears her
and then he asks them, what did she say? Did she say
this is a house of God? Swamiji heard it
as house of God. She said this God damn house or something
that's what she had said. Then the disciple
said, no no Swamiji, she is a drunkard and she is
a troublemaker like that. Anyway, and then this
ceremony went on. And then Swamiji
noticed that the boys are all there participating
in the ceremony and the two girls, Govindadasi
and Jadurani are not there.
So they were actually so upset that they were not included
for the ceremony. They stayed back in the apartment
a certain distance away and they were crying. Govindadasi
was crying that we are feeling left out. But then at some point
they made up their mind, what's this? You know we are going to miss Swamiji's
talk. And they decided, they kept aside and they came
running back. And the ceremony was going on and Prabhupada was still
talking. And then as they entered the room, so
gracious of Srila Prabhupada, Swamiji looked at them
and said, oh I was wondering, I was thinking about you.
Where were you? And Krishna has sent you.
You know so sweet of Srila Prabhupada to acknowledge those two young girls
who were feeling hurt like that. And Srila Prabhupada
said, oh Krishna has sent you. And so they sat down there
and they watched the ceremony going on and the whole ceremony happened.
And then the next day
you know, see you can imagine, Srila Prabhupada must have thought over
because those few days, few years, one or two years that went by
Prabhupada used to ask these young, his disciples Govinda Dasi and few others
understand about American culture, American society,
American practices, how women do
everything that men do. So Prabhupada
had heard about all those kind of things. So the next day
in the morning, Prabhupada calls Govinda Dasi
and her husband Gaur Sundar and has a talk.
And then he says, I have thought over
in America, women
can also get Gayatri Mantra and they can also
chant the Gayatri Mantra but they will not have the thread.
The previous day Prabhupada had given, draped a thread around
each one of them and made them chant Gayatri, how to chant Gayatri, all that had
happened. So Prabhupada said the next day he would conduct
the ceremony and the next day in the night, evening
they had another ceremony only for Govinda Dasi
and Jadurani. And both of them, Prabhupada
gave them Gayatri Mantra and he said in America
women can also do as much as men do. There is no difference

Om Namo Bhagavate Vasudevaya
Mantrane Mantra
Mantrena
Anena Devasya Kuryat
Parvyamaya Mayim Budhaha

Desha Kala Vibhagavit

This is the 12 syllable Mantra for worshipping Lord Krishna.
One should install the physical form of the Lord
and with the chanting of the Mantra, one should offer flowers
and fruits and other varieties of foodstuffs exactly

But this should be done
in consideration of place, time and attendant
conveniences and inconveniences. And Prabhupada
explains here now according to because it says Desha Kala

and Saparyam Vividhair Dravyaer
Desha Kala Vibhagavit. The paraphernalia Dravya
that you use should be according to Desha Kala, according to practical
circumstances and all of those kind of things. So you can see how Srila Prabhupada
according to time circumstances he was
presenting, he was
introducing all of these ancient Vedic
practices in ISKCON. And so
now you can see what is the meaning of Diksha
and what is the meaning of first initiation, what is the meaning
of second initiation, how Srila Prabhupada implemented all
of these things is a wonderful learning
and a wonderful understanding. With this we will stop here.
